info.title: Brilliant Directories API
info.description:
Universal API for Brilliant Directories websites. Manage members, content, leads, email templates, categories, and more. Works with any BD-powered site — just provide your site URL and API key.

## Authentication
All requests require an X-Api-Key header. Keys are created in BD Admin > Developer Hub > Generate API Key (see https://support.brilliantdirectories.com/support/solutions/articles/12000088768).

## Rate Limiting
Default limit: **100 requests per 60 seconds per API key.**

Customers can request a higher limit from Brilliant Directories support — any value between **100 and 1,000 requests per minute.** This is set server-side by BD on request; it is NOT a self-service setting in the BD admin. If you expect heavy API usage, contact BD support before bulk operations.

When the limit is exceeded, the API returns **HTTP 429 Too Many Requests**. Clients and AI agents should:
- For bulk operations (imports, batch updates, mass exports), pace requests below the configured limit
- On receiving 429, back off and retry after at least 60 seconds
- For known-large jobs, either ask BD support to raise the limit first OR process in batches with delays

The /api/v2/token/verify endpoint can be called before bulk jobs to confirm credentials work.

## Pagination
List endpoints use cursor-based pagination via limit + page params. Default page size is 25, max 100.

## Error Format
All errors: { "status": "error", "message": "..." } with standard HTTP codes (400 bad request, 401 unauthorized, 404 not found, 429 rate limited, 500 server error).

bd_site_url.description: The full URL of your BD-powered website (e.g. https://launch60031.directoryup.com)

apiKey.name: X-Api-Key
apiKey.description: Your BD API key. Generate in BD Admin > Developer Hub > Generate API Key.

page.name: page
page.description: Cursor token for pagination (use next_page value from previous response)

property.name: property
property.description: Field name to filter by (use property[] for multiple)

property_value.name: property_value
property_value.description: Value to filter by (use property_value[] for multiple)

property_operator.name: property_operator
property_operator.description: Filter operator: =, LIKE, >, <, >=, <= (use property_operator[] for multiple)

master_id.description: 0 for top-level, parent item ID for sub-items

get.description: Verify that your API key is valid and check rate limit status.
Returns available fields for user records with labels and required flags. Use this to discover custom fields.
Get a paginated list of all members. Supports filtering by any user field and sorting.

put.description: Update any user field. Supports special actions: member_tag_action + member_tags, credit_action + credit_amount, images_action, services, auto_geocode.

delete_images.description: Set to 1 to also delete user images

post.description: Full-text search across members with category, location, and sorting options.
Checks if email/password are valid. Does NOT return profile data — use getUser after.
Triggers automatic matching — system finds members matching category, location, and service area, then sends notification emails.

get.description: Returns custom fields for a specific post type form.

parameters.name: form_name
parameters.description: Form slug for the post type

post.summary: Get custom fields for a post type

post.description: Returns rendered HTML output with all template variables processed.

master_id.description: 0 for top-level, parent item ID for sub-items

post.summary: Link a service to a user

delete.summary: Remove a service from a user

get.description: Paginated list of all pages on the site: homepage, custom landing pages, profile/search templates, etc.

post.description: Create a new page record. seo_type and filename are required. IMPORTANT: after creating a page via API, the new URL will 404 on the public site until the user goes to BD Admin > Manage Pages and clicks Save on the record once. This triggers BD's URL router / cache rebuild. Always tell the user this step is needed.

seo_type.description: Page type identifier (home, profile, search, custom, etc.)

nickname.description: Human-readable label shown in admin panel

title.description: HTML title tag — supports template tokens like %%%website_name%%%

seo_text.description: Additional SEO body text rendered on page

content.description: Main HTML body — supports [widget=Name] shortcodes and template tokens

content_menu.description: Menu section this page belongs to

form_name.description: Form slug to render when show_form is enabled

facebook_title.description: Open Graph title for social sharing

page_render_widget.description: Widget ID to render as page content

seo_id.description: Page primary key (required to identify record)

seo_type.description: Page type identifier (home, profile, search, custom, etc.)

nickname.description: Human-readable label shown in admin panel

title.description: HTML title tag — supports template tokens like %%%website_name%%%

seo_text.description: Additional SEO body text rendered on page

content.description: Main HTML body — supports [widget=Name] shortcodes and template tokens

content_menu.description: Menu section this page belongs to

form_name.description: Form slug to render when show_form is enabled

facebook_title.description: Open Graph title for social sharing

page_render_widget.description: Widget ID to render as page content

get.description: Paginated list of all 301 redirect rules on the site.

post.description: Create a new 301 redirect rule. type, old_filename, and new_filename are required.

type.description: Category of the redirect source (profile, post, category, custom, etc.)

old_filename.description: The old URL path being redirected from, relative to the domain root (e.g. old-slug, not the full URL)

db_id.description: Database record ID of the source content object this redirect was generated from (0 if not tied to a record)

id.description: Legacy secondary identifier; typically 0 for system-generated redirects

redirect_id.description: Redirect primary key (required to identify record)

type.description: Category of the redirect source (profile, post, category, custom, etc.)

old_filename.description: The old URL path being redirected from, relative to the domain root (e.g. old-slug, not the full URL)

db_id.description: Database record ID of the source content object this redirect was generated from (0 if not tied to a record)

id.description: Legacy secondary identifier; typically 0 for system-generated redirects

tags.name: Users
tags.description: Members/users — create, update, search, login, transactions, subscriptions

tags.name: Post Types
tags.description: Post type categories and their custom fields

tags.name: Email Templates
tags.description: Email templates with merge tags and triggers

tags.name: User Services
tags.description: Links between users and services they offer

tags.name: Tag Types
tags.description: Tag type definitions and table relationships

tags.name: Smart Lists
tags.description: Dynamic filtered lists for members, leads, reviews, etc.

tags.name: Pages
tags.description: Static and SEO-enabled pages (homepage, about, landing pages, profile templates, etc.) — the list_seo resource

tags.name: Redirects
tags.description: 301 permanent redirect rules — preserve SEO when URLs change (profiles, posts, categories, custom rules)
